observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to
submit a list of unreleased checks at year-end to the Accounting Office for
the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to take up the necessary
adjustment to present the correct balances of cash accounts and
corresponding liability accounts.

We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal
Accountant to restate the financial statements by preparing the proposed
adjusting journal entries to restore cash balance and recognize the
corresponding payables in the appropriate cash and liability accounts.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Trace the cash advances granted to officers and employees for travel
purposes that were erroneously recorded under the Due from Officers
and Employees and other receivable accounts. These amounts should
be reclassified to the Advances to Officers and Employees account to
ensure accurate financial reporting;
b. Resolve the discrepancies noted and effect the corrections in the
subsequent Monthly Report of Cash Advances;
c. Prepare a JEV to correct the above-mentioned errors made in the
books of accounts; and
d. Prepare and maintain SLs covering the details or breakdowns of
balances of each controlling account and ensure that all cash advances
are accurately and completely recorded to avoid misleading
information in the financial statements.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that
Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to identify the affected accounts and
make the necessary adjusting journals entries in the books of the
agency;
b. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately transfer the
corresponding payable accounts from one fund to the other by availing
the services of their authorized depository bank through debit and
credit memo to ease the preparation of payment between intra-agency
accounts;
c. Require the Municipal Accountant to record in the Due from Other
Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts subsequent intra-agency
receivable and payable transactions; and
d. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
ensure that recording of deposits and disbursements are made in the
correct fund and to observe monthly reconciliation of book balances of
each depository bank accounts.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Restate the financial statements by preparing a JEV to correct the
errors made in the recognition of work accomplishment on the five
projects; and
b. Coordinate with the OIC-Municipal Engineer on the status of
infrastructure projects at year-end and draw the necessary JEV to
present the correct balances of the accounts presented in the financial
statements.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare a JEV to reclassify the liabilities incurred for personnel benefits,
travel expenses, donations, and reimbursements of other expenses of
officials and employees under the Due to Officers and Employees and
Other Payables accounts for proper presentation of the account balances
in the financial statements.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant observe the proper
use of accounts for each financial transaction to ensure proper recording
thereof to appropriate accounts.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to immediately transfer the CY
2024 unexpended LDRRMF amounting to ₱6,568,381.40 to the Trust
Fund books under Trust Liabilities - DRRMF account and the
subsequent year-end unexpended/unobligated balances of the
LDRRMF; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer
the unexpended balances of CY 2024 LDRRMF together with the
unutilized balances in previous years totaling ₱7,483,281.33
maintained in the Trust Fund regular bank account to the newly
opened bank account for LDRRMF for proper segregation of funds.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
take-up the reclassifying journal entry to present the correct balances of
the affected liability accounts.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant, in
coordination with the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer, to:
a. Conduct a thorough reconciliation between the SCBAA and Statement
of Financial Performance and ensure that all reconciling items for non-
cash items and timing differences are correctly accounted and disclosed
in the Notes to the FS to provide reliable information to the users of the
financial statements;
b. Restate the CY 2024 SCBAA by incorporating the necessary
adjustments to address the noted discrepancy of ₱27,541,819.79
between the ending balance of CY 2023 and the beginning balance
reported in CY 2024, to accurately present the beginning balance to be
forwarded in the CY 2025 SCBAA; and
c. Review and validate all carry-over balances for Continuing
Appropriations to ensure accurate and consistent reporting in the
subsequent fiscal years.

We recommended that The Municipal Accountant prepare an adjusting journal entry in journal entry to record the stale checks amounting to  ₱32,727.62 to Accounts Payable.

We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to notify the payee once a check has been drawn, to check for its replacement.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant recorded an adjusting journal under JEV No. 101-24-07-02

The Municipal Treasurer notified the payees to surrender the stale check.

Municipal Accountant has identified the affected accounts, and direct transfer was prepared from payable account from one fund to the other.

The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant transferred the corresponding payable accounts from one fund to the other.

Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts for subsequent intra-agency receivables and payable transactions had been used in compliance with the recommendation of the Commission.

The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant ensures that all deposits and disbursements are recorded under the correct fund and conduct monthly reconciliations of book balances with each depository bank account to identify and resolve discrepancies promptly.

Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended balances of CY 2024 LDRRMF together with the unutilized balances in previous years totaling ₱7,483,281.33 maintained in the Trust Fund regular bank account to the newly opened bank account for LDRRMF for proper segregation of funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer, to:

Conduct a thorough reconciliation between the SCBAA and Statement of Financial Performance and ensure that all reconciling items for non-cash items and timing differences are correctly accounted and disclosed in the Notes to the FS to provide reliable information to the users of the financial statements; 

Restate the CY 2024 SCBAA by incorporating the necessary adjustments to address the noted discrepancy of ₱27,541,819.79 between the ending balance of CY 2023 and the beginning balance reported in CY 2024, to accurately present the beginning balance to be forwarded in the CY 2025 SCBAA; and

Review and validate all carry-over balances for Continuing Appropriations to ensure accurate and consistent reporting in the subsequent fiscal years.
observations.managementAction:

Carry-over balances for Continuing Appropriations have been reviewed and validated and will be reflected in the beginning balance of SCBAA for CY 2025 to ensure the accurate and consistent reporting in the succeeding fiscal years and form part of the year end closing procedure.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE, who is tasked under the law to be primarily responsible in safeguarding government funds, to:

Institute actions to strictly enforce the immediate settlement/liquidation of cash advances of accountable officials/employees who are still in service

Exhaust all possible means to collect the cash advances from previous employees who are no longer in the service, otherwise proceed against the signatories of the clearance, who are still in the service;

Request authority to write off from the Commission on Audit; and

Require the officers and employees to refrain from securing cash advances other than travel.
observations.managementAction:
Management had already sent demand letters to employees who are still in service to settle their unliquidated cash advances.



Final demand letters were sent to previous employees who are no longer in service.





Request authority to write off from the Commission on Audit will be considered when cash advances are still uncollected after management efforts are exhausted.

Only cash disbursing officers are authorized to secure cash advances other than for travel. Officers and employees other than cash disbursing officers are allowed to cash advance for travel purposes only.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the:

GSO to conduct inventory semesterly and submit the report to the Audit Team every July 31st and January 31st of each year;


Municipal Accountant to use the Perpetual Inventory Method, wherein the supplies or materials purchased shall be coursed through the appropriate inventory account and the corresponding expense account shall be debited upon issuance based on the SSMI; and

GSO to maintain stock cards for all items under inventory and undertake periodic reconciliation of records with that of the Municipal Accountant to avoid any misstatements in the quantity and amount.
observations.managementAction:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to record in the books the 23 parcels of land owned by the LGU amounting to ₱51,779,920.00 with supporting documents.
We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Assessor to gather all the needed documents in establishing the legal ownership over the 26 parcels of land covered by Tax Declaration only through a Certificate of Title in the name of the LGU in order to protect its interest for any adverse claims by third parties.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to record the appropriate expense accounts corresponding to the amount disbursed and the appropriate income account equivalent to the amount utilized in the total amount of ₱17,657,575.00 in order to present fairly the income and expense accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure compliance with the generally accepted accounting principle and reconcile her records with that of the Municipal Treasurer by analyzing the affected transactions to determine the cause/s of the negative balance in the Cash-Local Treasury account under the Trust Fund amounting to ₱1,000.00 and effect the necessary adjustment in the books.
We recommended that Management require the accountable person to render an accounting of the funds received from the LGU and undertake the necessary measures to collect the receivables.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to revert the accounts payable which remained outstanding for more than two years and which no actual claims exist to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund in the total amount of ₱103,461.76.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit a copy of the relief distribution sheets indicating the list of recipients for each barangay validated by the MDRRMO and proof of receipt of relief goods by the Punong Barangays distributed to the recipients.

We recommended that the Management limit the hiring of experts to its actual need of consultancy services, taking into consideration the specific work requiring highly specialized or technical expertise in a field of special knowledge or training that cannot be done by the regular staff of the LGU.

We recommended that Management require the drivers to accomplish the driver’s trip tickets properly by indicating therein all the required information.  We, likewise, recommended that Management instruct the designated personnel of the LGU to prepare the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from obligating and charging prior year’s expenditure to current year’s appropriation.  Proper coordination must be observed among municipal officials and employees in order that expenses are obligated and taken up as Accounts Payable for the current year but are to be paid in the succeeding year.

We recommended that Management require the BAC to submit the mandatory documents in the use of alternative method of Small Value and to ensure that the most advantageous price is obtained from suppliers of known qualifications. We also recommended that Management instruct the BAC, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to see to it that all of the documentary requirements are duly dated and complete to ensure transparency and propriety of the procurement transactions.

We recommended that Management instruct the GSO and the Inspectorate Team to ensure that the Acceptance and Inspection Reports are properly filled up and accomplished to fully establish the compliance of the supplier with the terms of the contract or purchase order before the approval of payment.
We further recommended that Management instruct the GSO to require the supplier to issue delivery receipt upon delivery of the goods and require the GSO to sign the acceptance portion thereof, with the date duly indicated therein.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to accept Sales Invoice or Official Receipts as evidence of payment.  Collection receipt may be accepted only when charge invoice was initially issued by the supplier.
We, likewise, recommended that Management strictly adhere to the existing regulations to ensure that transactions incurred by the LGU are properly substantiated.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to apply the remedies provided under the law in order to generate more revenue that may be appropriated for the implementation of additional programs and projects for the benefit of its constituents and enforce the sanctions for non-compliance therewith.  We, likewise, recommended that Management may consider acquiring a computerized system for effective and efficient monitoring of real property taxes.

We reiterated the recommendation that Management formulate and immediately implement a long-term solution leading to a self-reliant LGU, as mandated by the Local Government Code and Republic Act No. 9003, to address its solid wastes for the benefit of the local constituents.

We recommended that Management immediately transfer the unexpended/unobligated balances of 2020 QRF and DRRMF-MOOE to the Special Trust Fund for easy monitoring of disaster risk reduction and management fund.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to promptly remit all collections and premiums withheld from the salaries of municipal employees to the GSIS, PhilHealth and HDMF.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to require the refund of the amount paid by the LGU in excess of the mandatory government share in PAG-IBIG contributions in the total amount of ₱668,780.38.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to remit the taxes withheld to the BIR within the reglementary period in order to avoid payment of surcharges, interests and penalty.  We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer immediately remit the amount of ₱1,864,178.54 to the BIR.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit a list/schedule showing the details of the accounts payable reverted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure the proper management and accounting of its properties by undertaking the following measures:
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all Property, Plant and Equipment, prepare an Inventory Report which shall be reconciled with the accounting records and submit a copy to the Auditor not later than January 31, of each year.
Instruct the Accountant to keep complete property, plant and equipment ledger cards or subsidiary ledgers which should reconcile with the corresponding control account of the Property, Plant and Equipment account in the general ledger.
Require the Property Custodian to keep records of all properties of the Municipality, maintain property cards and prepare Property Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (PAR) for every property issued to employees and renewed every after three years of issue.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the MGD Head/General Services Officer to furnish the GSIS with a copy of the latest inventory report with complete list of all insurable properties so that the appraisal thereof and the computation of the amount of insurance premiums payable by the Municipality could be readily made. Strict compliance with R.A. No. 656 should be observed to ensure that the Municipality will be compensated or indemnified for property damages or losses caused by fire, earthquake and other covered calamities. The Municipal Budget Officer should likewise provide appropriation for the insurance of the insurable properties of the Municipality.

We recommended that management recalculate and set up a residual value of five (5) percent of the cost as well as apply the necessary adjusting entries to correct the accumulated depreciation in order to reflect the accurate and fair amount of Property, Plant and Equipment accounts presented in the financial statements that will provide reliable information to users of information and in accordance with PPSAS 3 and PPSAS 17.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer to submit the lacking documents for technical review and evaluation by the COA TAS in order to establish the validity of the claim thereof.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to give priority in tracing back the historical costs or values used in recording the properties in the books of the agency. The GSO should likewise maintain real property ledger cards to be reconciled with the control accounts and physical inventory. Any discrepancies noted should be immediately verified and adjusted. Moreover, the GSO should maintain property ledger cards to be reconciled with the inventory report and that the Office of the Municipal Accountant should likewise maintain real property ledger cards to be reconciled with the control accounts and physical inventory. Any discrepancies noted should be immediately verified and adjusted. Moreover, the GSO should coordinate with the Office of the Municipal Accountant on the latest acquisition/construction of Land and Buildings for the purpose of completing/updating the inventory report.

We recommended that  the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee ensure approval of Construction Safety and Health Program by the Department of Labor and Employment (DOLE) in compliance with Section 37.2.3 (f) of Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act No. 9184 (R.A. 9184).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher for the adjustments of the correct amount to be taken up in the books based on the data on CIP for completed projects.  Likewise, there must be assigned personnel to maintain CIP ledger cards for each project under construction either by straight contract or by administration, including the Registries of Public Infrastructure for each class of completed project for general public use.  Public Infrastructure accounts funded by loans should be closed only to Government Equity account upon payment of the loan.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MPDC to give priority in implementing projects under the 20% development fund to attain a desirable socio-economic development and environment outcomes for the constituents in accordance with the provisions of DILG and DBM Joint Memo Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to:
●	Immediately deposit/remit the full amount of ₱6,259,114.44 to the account GSIS/BIR/Pag-Ibig/Philhealth and National Treasury in accordance with E.O.No. 338, as implemented by COA-DBM-DOF Joint Circular No. 1-97 dated January 2, 1997. Likewise, enforce the immediate remittance of the full amount of ₱5,392,474.31 of the account “Due to LGUs as required in the NGAS Manual.
●	Ensure that collections of trust receipts are deposited intact to the AGDB at end of the month to safeguards said funds from risks of loss and/or misappropriation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to remit/release RPT/SET shares to the concerned LGUs  in order to prevent its use for other purpose and for the immediate appropriation of the funds for projects//programs beneficial to the Province and barangay constituents.  Henceforth, see to it that their shares from RPT/ SET collections be regularly remitted within the prescribed period as provided under Section 271 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure strict compliance with the liquidation of all cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002; and to cause the immediate settlement of the traveling/seminar expenses of the concerned officials and employees by ordering the withholding of their salaries or any money due them. However, in the case of outstanding cash advances of deceased employees, the head of the agency shall initiate the filing of the request for write-off from the COA Audit Team Leader and /or Supervising Auditor pursuant to paragraph 8.2 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to adopt or design a better policy in granting loans to ensure its collection. The loan agreement must be revised, wherein the date of maturity must be indicated, payment must not be on a voluntary basis and imposition of interest and penalty must be strict. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should be required to issue demand letters to debtors stating therein the principal as well as the accumulated interest.  In case where the debtors fail to settle their accounts after due notices, apply legal sanctions.  This may help to improve the collection efficiency of the municipality wherein the funds collected may be utilized for re-loaning to other beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the provisions of Section 32  of  NGAS Manual for LGUs whereby all collections should be deposited intact and daily or not later than the next banking day with the  authorized depository bank of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Accounting Unit of the Municipality maintain complete Registries of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations per function/program/project, allotment class, current/continuing appropriations and fund to monitor appropriations, allotments received and obligations incurred and ensure that the balance is extracted every time an entry is made to prevent incurrence of obligations in excess of allotments or approved appropriations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the General Services Officer to procure as far as practicable and feasible, directly from the PS-DBM all anticipated common-use supplies, and resort to local suppliers only upon unavailability of supplies needed using the prescribed alternative modes of procurement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant maintain sub-accounts consisting of separate ledgers for the enterprise and consequently prepare trial balances and financial statements reflecting the financial condition and results of their respective operations in accordance with existing regulations.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of utilizing the fund appropriated for the implementation of the programs of the Local Councils for the protection of children (LPDC) for any purpose beyond those expressly prescribed by the above cited regulation.  The Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate the amount of 1 per cent(1%) of its Internal Revenue Allotment(IRA) in accordance with Section 15, Title III of Chapter I of Republic Act No. 9344.

We recommended that the General Services Officer and other officials/offices concerned furnish the Audit Team with copies of contracts/purchase orders and supporting documents and Acceptance and Inspection Reports as required by COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 95-006. This is designed to enable the Audit Team to perform its duties relating to post-audit of the transactions, at the most appropriate time and manner without delaying or obstructing the implementation or prosecution of the project.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of Section 47 of the General Provisions of Republic Act No. 10924, General Appropriations Act  for Fiscal Year 2017 by screening the loan application of its employees so that the monthly net take home pay will not be reduced to an amount below P4,000.00 after all the authorized deductions. Also, restructure the deductions particularly debt servicing of the concerned employees to conform to the required amount of net take home pay.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Assessor to update and modernize its tax recording and valuation and bring to light the existing properties of the Municipality. Enjoin the municipal officials concerned to strictly adhere to Section 212 of the Local Government Code of 1991 for proper, efficient and effective administration of real property taxes.

We recommended that the Management strictly comply with the provisions of the Joint Circular No. 2012-01 of PCW-NEDA-DBM  and Executive Order No. 273 in order to ensure that proper planning and budgeting is provided for he GAD program and activities which will address the Gender related issues and concerns identified in the GAD Plan of the agency. Also, we require the agency GFPS to prepare the annual GAD accomplishment report following the guidelines indicated in the Joint Circular. Moreover, they should stop from charging unrelated expenditures against the GAD fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the LDRRMO to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA Auditor of the LGU on or before the 15th day after the end of each month in compliance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002. The LDRRMO incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the detailed planned projects/programs/activities chargeable to the unexpected LDRRMF of previous years in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief executive adopt the following measures to improve its collections, to wit:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to intensify /improve their collection strategies on taxes due and collectible by sending RPTOP’s and notice of delinquency to taxpayers and forming committee to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes.
If warranted, impose the provisions of Sections 258 and 260 of the Local Government Code of 1991 in the collections of delinquent taxes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to come up with an effective collection strategy to minimize delinquencies, and to impose the 25% surcharge on rentals not paid within the first twenty (20) days each month in accordance with Tax Ordinance No. 1, Series of 2011. In addition, direct the Market Supervisor to strictly monitor the implementation of the market policies to improve its collections.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the appropriate management mechanism of the Municipal Health Office optimally utilize the Per Family Payment Rate Fund in accordance with the utilization set forth under Philhealth Circular No. 10, series of 2012 to ensure the constituents better access to quality health services.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer in coordination with the Inventory Committee, immediately inspect the Timber Bridge not only to ascertain its actual condition but also to determine the appraise realizable value which are prerequisites to final disposal. Prompt disposal shall be initiated to prevent further deterioration and loss and consequently realize higher proceeds from the disposal. The materials may be used by the LGU for projects beneficial to its constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Office of the Municipal Accountant to reconcile its records on taxes due on compensation and all money payments to suppliers, contractors and other creditors and the Municipal Treasurer to promptly/ immediately remit the same to the Bureau of Internal Revenue pursuant to BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-98 in order to contribute to the government’s revenue generation efforts. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should prepare the necessary documents to facilitate the immediate remittance of the unremitted taxes.  Henceforth, all remittances shall be checked to ensure that these tally with the taxes withheld.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer to strictly monitor all on-going projects, prepare a monthly monitoring report on infrastructure projects and submit the same to the concerned department /office for information and /or appropriate action.

We have recommended  that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to transfer the unexpended balance of LDRRMF for 2016 to the Special Trust Fund in compliance with Republic Act No. 10121 and Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We have recommended that Management submit immediately to the Audit Team the required documents. Henceforth, all concerned shall follow strictly the mandates on complete documentation as required under relevant rules, in the process, preclude the suspension or disallowance of transactions during post-audit.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive  require the Municipal Treasurer to remit/release RPT/SET shares to the concerned LGUs  in order to prevent its use for other purpose and for the immediate appropriation of the funds for projects//programs beneficial to the Province and barangay constituents.  Henceforth, see to it that their shares from RPT/ SET collections be regularly remitted within the prescribed period as provided under Section 271 of RA 7160.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive ensure strict compliance with the liquidation of all cash advances pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002; and to cause the immediate settlement of the traveling/seminar expenses of the concerned officials and employees by ordering the withholding of their salaries or any money due them. However, in the case of outstanding cash advances of deceased employees, the head of the agency shall initiate the filing of the request for write-off from the COA Audit Team Leader and /or Supervising Auditor pursuant to paragraph 8.2 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to adopt or design a better policy in granting loans to ensure its collection. The loan agreement must be revised, wherein the date of maturity must be indicated, payment must not be on a voluntary basis and imposition of interest and penalty must be strict. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should be required to issue demand letters to debtors stating therein the principal as well as the accumulated interest.  In case where the debtors fail to settle their accounts after due notices, apply legal sanctions. This may help to improve the collection efficiency of the municipality wherein the funds collected may be utilized for re-loaning to other beneficiaries.

We  reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation  that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the provisions of Section 32  of  NGAS Manual for LGUs whereby all collections should be deposited intact and daily or not later than the next banking day with the  authorized depository bank of the Municipality.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant maintain sub-accounts consisting of separate ledgers for the enterprise and consequently prepare trial balances and financial statements reflecting the financial condition and results of their respective operations in accordance with existing regulations.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive ensure compliance with A.O. No. 17 in the procurement of common-use supplies, and resort to the Alternative Modes of procurement only upon the certification from the PS-DBM that the supplies subject of procurement is not available.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit copies of Perfected Contracts, bidding documents and other supporting documents to the Auditor within the period prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-001 to effect timely review and audit of the contracts and related documents.

We have recommended that management require:
The Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to payees of checks which has long been outstanding and the need to surrender checks for cancellation, which became stale in the hands of the payees.
The OIC-Municipal Accountant to adjust the affected accounts involved by debiting the Cash in Bank to reflect its actual balances and crediting the Accounts Payable to restore the liability to concerned creditors.
Strict adherence to existing rules and regulations regarding the disposition of stale checks should be observed.

We have recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of Section 52 of the General Appropriations Act, FY 2016 by screening the loan application of its employees so that the monthly net take home pay will not be reduced to an amount below ₱3,500.00 after all the authorized deductions. Also, restructure the deductions particularly debt servicing of the concerned employees to conform to the required amount of net take home pay.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Office of the GSO to properly mark all vehicles owned by the Municipality as prescribed under COA Circular No. 75-6 and ensure that all trips made thereon are properly monitored with corresponding documentations.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Accounting Unit of the Municipality maintain complete Registries of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations per function/program/project, allotment class, current/continuing appropriations and fund to monitor appropriations, allotments received and obligations incurred and ensure that the balance is extracted every time an entry is made to prevent incurrence of obligations in excess of allotments or approved appropriations. Also, the Municipal Budget Officer should use the Obligation Request instead of Obligation Slip.

We have recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant take appropriate action necessary of the dormant account. If it can no longer be accounted, a request for write off and/or adjustment be made subject to the requirement of COA Circular No. 97-001.
We have recommended that  Management stop from charging expenses not in accordance with the PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01, The  GAD Planning and Budgeting Guidelines.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief executive adopt the following measures to improve its collections, to wit:
Require the Municipal Treasurer to intensify /improve their collection strategies on taxes due and collectible by sending RPTOP’s and notice of delinquency to taxpayers and forming committee to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes.
If warranted, impose the provisions of Sections 258 and 260 of the Local Government Code of 1991 in the collections of delinquent taxes.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to come up with an effective collection strategy to minimize delinquencies, and to impose the 25% surcharge on rentals not paid within the first twenty (20) days each month in accordance with Tax Ordinance No. 1, Series of 2011. In addition, direct the Market Supervisor to strictly monitor the implementation of the market policies to improve its collections.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the appropriate management mechanism of the Municipal Health Office optimally utilize the Per Family Payment Rate Fund in accordance with the utilization set forth under Philhealth Circular No. 10, series of 2012 to ensure the constituents better access to quality health services.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer in coordination with the Inventory Committee, immediately inspect the Timber Bridge not only to ascertain its actual condition but also to determine the appraise realizable value which are prerequisites to final disposal. Prompt disposal shall be initiated to prevent further deterioration and loss and consequently realize higher proceeds from the disposal. The materials may be used by the LGU for projects beneficial to its constituents.

We have recommended that management continue to collect and remit taxes to the national government on time. However, the Accounting Office should be advised to be vigilant so as not to incur under/over-remittance of taxes withheld.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the immediate settlement of all disallowances that has become final and executory; and to withhold the salary on any money claim against the persons held liable and for which a Notice of Finality has already been issued, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure the proper management and accounting of its properties by undertaking the following measures:

Create an Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all Property, Plant and Equipment, prepare an Inventory Report which shall be reconciled with the accounting records and submit a copy to the Auditor not later than January 31, of each year. 

Instruct the Accountant to keep complete property, plant and equipment ledger cards or subsidiary ledgers which should reconcile with the corresponding control account of the Property, Plant and Equipment account in the general ledger.

Require the Property Custodian to keep records of all properties of the Municipality, maintain property cards and prepare Property Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (PAR) for every property issued to employees and renewed every after three years of issue.
